perturbation.original_text: a. Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer. In consideration for the rights, services and benefits granted by North hereunder, Company agrees to pay North a non-refundable Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer (hereinafter referred to as "Guaranteed Fee") of fifty-five thousand dollars ($55,000USD) in Contract Year 2011 and seventy thousand dollars ($70,000USD) in Contract Year 2012.
perturbation.changed_text: a. Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer. In consideration for the rights, services and benefits granted by North hereunder, Company agrees to pay North a non-refundable Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer (hereinafter referred to as "Guaranteed Fee") as outlined in subsequent addendums.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific Guaranteed Fee amounts for 2011 and 2012, but still referring to 'Guaranteed Fee' in later sections, creates an in-text contradiction. The amendment suggests these amounts will be specified in addendums, but there are no addendums specifying these fees included in the provided contract text, which introduces uncertainty in enforcement. The section 7.a.(i), (ii), (iii) and 7.a. (i),(ii),(iii), which specify how much the payment will be in certain dates, directly contradicts with this omission.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
a. Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer. In consideration for the rights, services and benefits granted by North hereunder, Company agrees to pay North a non-refundable Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer (hereinafter referred to as "Guaranteed Fee") as outlined in subsequent addendums.
(i) Twenty-Five thousand dollars ($25,000USD) on or before two days after this Agreement is fully executed. (ii) Fifteen thousand dollars ($15,000USD) on or before July 1, 2011. (iii) Fifteen thousand dollars ($ 15,000USD) on or before November 1, 2011. (i) Twenty-Five thousand dollars ($25,000USD) on or before February 1, 2012 (ii) Twenty-Five thousand dollars ($25,000USD) on or before July 1, 2012 (iii) Twenty thousand dollars ($20,000USD) on or before November 1, 2012
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the exact dollar amounts for the Guaranteed Service Fee in 2011 and 2012. The changed text removes these specific amounts, stating they are 'as outlined in subsequent addendums'. However, the contradicted text specifies precise dollar amounts and dates for payments throughout 2011 and 2012, directly contradicting the amended version's implication that these amounts would be detailed in addendums. Because the change replaces fixed amounts with a reference to unspecified addendums while existing clauses specify fixed payments, the agreement introduces a clear inconsistency, creating ambiguity as to the actual payment schedule. The modified text directly conflicts with the contradicted text, thus creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: b. Royalties. In addition to payments due North by Company as set forth in paragraphs 7.a. above, Company further agrees to pay North a one percent (1%) royalty on all Gross Revenue generated from the sale of all Company Products (herein "Royalties").
perturbation.changed_text: b. Royalties. In addition to payments due North by Company as set forth in paragraphs 7.a. above, Company further agrees to pay North a royalty on all Gross Revenue generated from the sale of all Company Products (herein "Royalties"). The specific percentage will be determined based on Company's yearly performance.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific royalty percentage (1%) but still referring to 'Royalties' in later sections, and adding the condition of determining based on company's yearly performance introduces uncertainty. Section 7.c.(i), which previously contained the royalty payment method, now refers to a rate not clearly defined, creating an internal contradiction, because the royalty should be based on the company's performance, instead of a 1% which it doesn't exist anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c. Payment of Royalties/Statement of Accounts. (i) Company agrees to pay all royalties due as set forth in section 7.b. above on or before thirty (30) days following the end of each quarterly period during the Contract Period. The quarterly royalty payment shall be accompanied by a statement of account indicating the quantities of Products sold, the Gross Revenue generated from all sales of the Product where the sales were derived from (i.e. direct sales or retail sales) and the royalty payment that is due to North.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 1% royalty in section 7.b. The changed text removes the specific percentage and makes the royalty percentage dependent on the company's yearly performance. Section 7.c.(i) refers back to section 7.b. for the royalty rate. Because 7.b. no longer contains a fixed rate, but instead a variable one based on performance, this contradicts the implied assumption in 7.c.(i) that a definite royalty rate is known and payable, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: North agrees that any Advertising Materials submitted for approval as provided herein may be deemed by Company to have been approved hereunder if the same is not disapproved by North within seven (7) business days after receipt thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: North agrees that any Advertising Materials submitted for approval as provided herein may be deemed by Company to have been approved hereunder, subject to North's reasonable discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the 'seven (7) business days' timeframe for disapproval of Advertising Materials introduces an in-text contradiction. Previously, a lack of response within that timeframe was considered approval. Now, approval is subject to 'North's reasonable discretion', which contradicts section 9, because it does not consider that the advertising materials will be approved if North doesn't disapprove in a timely manner, which leads to ambiguity in the approval process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Furthermore, subject to North's approval as set forth in section 8 above, Company shall have the right at any time during the Term of this Agreement to make any revisions to the Advertising Materials or prepare versions of all or any part of the Advertising Materials to conform to the requirements of individual markets or customers.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a seven-day timeframe for disapproval, implying approval if no action is taken. The changed text removes this timeframe and introduces 'North's reasonable discretion,' contradicting the contradicted text, which anticipates North's approval 'as set forth in section 8.' The removal of the timeframe in the changed text introduces uncertainty and creates an inconsistency about how North's approval is determined, as the approval is no longer time bound. The 'reasonable discretion' aspect now introduces a subjective element that conflicts with the expectation of a timely, if implicit, approval in the original clause.
